Class Player

java.lang.Object
com.example.elcincuentazo.model.Player
Direct Known Subclasses:
HumanPlayer, MachinePlayer
public abstract class Player extends Object
Abstract base class representing a participant in a Cincuentazo game.

A player holds a PlayerHand of up to 4 cards and can be either active (still in the game) or eliminated. Concrete subclasses define whether the player is human (HumanPlayer) or machine (MachinePlayer).

See Also:
  • HumanPlayer
  • MachinePlayer

    Constructs a new player with the given name.

    Indicates whether this player is human or machine-controlled.

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Player

      public Player(String name)
      Constructs a new player with the given name. The player starts with an empty hand and is active by default.
      Parameters:
      name - display name for this player; must not be null

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the display name of this player.
      Returns:
      player name

    • getHand

      public PlayerHand getHand()
      Returns the hand of cards held by this player.
      Returns:
      this player's PlayerHand

    • isActive

      public boolean isActive()
      Returns true if the player is still in the game.
      Returns:
      true when active

    • eliminate

      public void eliminate()
      Marks this player as eliminated (sets active to false). Should only be called by GameLogic after the elimination condition has been confirmed.

    • hasPlayableCard

      public boolean hasPlayableCard(int currentSum)
      Checks whether at least one card in this player's hand can legally be played without causing the table sum to exceed 50.
      Parameters:
      currentSum - the current sum on the table
      Returns:
      true if there is at least one playable card

    • getCards

      public List<Card> getCards()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the cards currently in this player's hand. Convenience wrapper around PlayerHand.getCards().
      Returns:
      list of cards

    • receiveCard

      public void receiveCard(Card card)
      Adds a card to this player's hand (e.g. after drawing from the deck).
      Parameters:
      card - the card to add; must not be null

    • playCard

      public Card playCard(int index, int currentSum) throws InvalidCardPlayException
      Plays the card at the given hand index. Validates the play against currentSum before removing the card.
      Parameters:
      index - zero-based index of the card to play
      currentSum - the current table sum
      Returns:
      the played Card
      Throws:
      InvalidCardPlayException - if the card would cause the sum to exceed 50
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is out of range

    • playCard

      public Card playCard(Card card, int currentSum) throws InvalidCardPlayException
      Plays a specific card instance from this player's hand.
      Parameters:
      card - the card to play
      currentSum - the current table sum
      Returns:
      the played Card
      Throws:
      InvalidCardPlayException - if the play is illegal
      IllegalArgumentException - if the card is not in this player's hand

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a display-friendly description of this player's state.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      formatted string with name, status and hand contents

    • isHuman

      public abstract boolean isHuman()
      Indicates whether this player is human or machine-controlled. Subclasses must implement this to allow the engine to differentiate between GUI interaction and AI decision-making.
      Returns:
      true for a human player, false for a machine
